Dropdown menu toggle does not announce state changes via a live region
Product‑filter view‑mode toggle button does not announce layout change via a live region
Product‑filter sort `<select>` change does not announce the new sort order via a live region
Search input (`#search-input`) lacks `aria-controls` linking to the suggestion list
Search component does not announce result‑count updates via a dedicated live region
Product‑filter view‑mode toggle button lacks an accessible name (`aria-label` or visible text)
Sort `<select>` element lacks an accessible label (`aria-label` or associated `<label>`)
Filter‑tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) lack proper ARIA tab roles (`role="tab"` and `role="tablist"` on the container)
Filter‑tab buttons do not announce activation via a live region when a new category is selected
Toggle function `mobileNav.toggle` does not set **aria‑controls** on the hamburger button when the drawer element lacks an `id`.
Toggle function `dropdownMenu._openInstance` hides/shows a dropdown panel but never adds **aria‑controls** to the trigger when the panel has no `id`.
Toggle function `pricing.setPeriod` updates the visual state of the billing‑period buttons but does **not** set **aria‑pressed** on the button that acts as a simple on/off switch (`_toggleEl`).
Toggle function `productFilter._viewToggle` (the “grid/list” view buttons) updates CSS classes but does **not** set **aria‑pressed** on the buttons when they are activated.

Dynamic content update `productFilter.applyFilters` changes the visible product count and empty‑state message but does **not** write to a dedicated live‑region for the count change. It only uses `liveRegion.announce`, which may be overridden by other announcements.

Validation function `contactForm._onSubmit` marks fields invalid via `inlineValidator.validate`, which sets `aria-invalid`, but when the form is successfully submitted it never **clears** `aria-invalid` on fields that were previously invalid.
Visibility‑toggle function `mobileNav._close` hides the overlay (`_overlay`) by adding a CSS class and later setting `hidden`, but never sets **aria‑hidden** on the overlay element.
Visibility‑toggle function `pricing._updateSavingsMessage` hides the savings banner with `setAttribute('hidden')` but does **not** set **aria‑hidden**.

Dynamic content update `testimonialsCarousel._onPrev/_onNext` changes the visible slide and announces it via `liveRegion.announce`, but the slide number is not written to a dedicated live‑region element that is always present (the carousel lacks a persistent status region).

Dynamic content update `productFilter._syncUrl` modifies the URL hash and query parameters but does **not** announce the navigation change to screen‑reader users (e.g., when the filter changes the page URL).

Toggle function `fileUploadField._addFile` adds files to the upload list and announces via `liveRegion.announce`, but the upload list itself lacks an **aria‑label** or **role="list"** to convey its purpose.
Visibility‑toggle function `productFilter._updateEmptyState` hides the empty‑state element with `setAttribute('hidden')` but does not set **aria‑hidden**.

Dynamic content update `pricing._updatePlanCard` changes the price text but does **not** write the new price to a live‑region for screen‑reader users (the price element receives an `aria-label`, but the label is not announced because no live region is used).

Toggle function `multiStepWizard._onNext` validates the current step and, on success, moves to the next step but does **not** announce the step change via a dedicated live‑region (it only updates `aria-selected` on tabs).

column1: # FALSE NEGATIVE DETAIL  (real issues the model failed to detect)
# CONCEPT MISS HEATMAP  (how many models missed each expected issue — higher = harder to detect)
# COMPLEXITY REGRESSION  (F1 degradation across low / medium / high fixtures)
# STREAMING QUALITY  (F1 at partial token percentages — plateau = earliest % reaching 90% of final F1)
# PARETO FRONTIER  (models where no competitor has both better F1 and lower latency)
# ADVERSARIAL VULNERABILITY  (detection rate per violation type on adversarial fixtures)
